prompt: Fix the JWT middleware — it rejects valid tokens

turns.content:
<task>
Fix the JWT auth middleware — it returns 401 for valid tokens. Users report being logged out randomly.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Line 12: `header.split(' ')[0]` extracts 'Bearer' instead of the actual token. Authorization header format is 'Bearer <token>', so index should be [1] not [0]. This would cause jwt.verify to fail on the word 'Bearer', returning 401.

turns.content:
PASS tests/auth.test.ts
  JWT Auth Middleware
    ✓ rejects requests without token (8 ms)
    ✓ rejects invalid tokens (5 ms)
    ✓ accepts valid Bearer tokens (6 ms)
    ✓ sets req.user from token payload (4 ms)

Tests: 4 passed, 4 total
Time: 2.1s

arguments.summary: Fixed JWT middleware in src/middlewares/jwt-auth.ts:12 — was splitting Authorization header and taking index [0] ('Bearer') instead of [1] (the actual token). jwt.verify was failing on the literal string 'Bearer'. One-character fix: [0] -> [1]. All 4 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed JWT auth middleware at src/middlewares/jwt-auth.ts. The Authorization header split was taking index [0] (the 'Bearer' prefix) instead of [1] (the token). This caused all valid tokens to be rejected. All 4 tests pass.
